Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Graphics Programming Resources 144 upvotes, 13 comments
  2. Show HN: I made a zero-copy coroutine tracer to find my scheduler's lost wakeups 36 upvotes, 1 comments
  3. Textadept 175 upvotes, 33 comments
  4. Show HN: Stacked Game of Life 94 upvotes, 17 comments
  5. Elevator Saga: The elevator programming game (2015) 50 upvotes, 7 comments
  6. Modern Illustration: Archive of illustration from c.1950-1975 25 upvotes, 2 comments
  7. Voxile: A ray-traced game made in its own engine and programming language 241 upvotes, 65 comments
  8. Charging a three-cell nickel-based battery pack with a Li-Ion charger [pdf] 10 upvotes, 0 comments
  9. Greg Knauss Is Losing Himself 19 upvotes, 2 comments
  10. Motorola GrapheneOS devices will be bootloader unlockable/relockable 982 upvotes, 390 comments
  11. Claude's Cycles [pdf] 701 upvotes, 294 comments
  12. Qwen3.5 Fine-Tuning Guide – Unsloth Documentation 30 upvotes, 7 comments
  13. Apple Announces Low-Cost 'MacBook Neo' with A18 Pro Chip 29 upvotes, 7 comments
  14. On the Design of Programming Languages (1974) [pdf] 64 upvotes, 22 comments
  15. RE#: how we built the fastest regex engine in F# 107 upvotes, 42 comments
  16. RFC 9849. TLS Encrypted Client Hello 169 upvotes, 74 comments
  17. Better JIT for Postgres 100 upvotes, 40 comments
  18. Chimpanzees Are into Crystals 27 upvotes, 7 comments
  19. Agentic Engineering Patterns 295 upvotes, 151 comments
  20. Did Alibaba just kneecap its powerful Qwen AI team? 45 upvotes, 17 comments
  21. Libre Solar – Open Hardware for Renewable Energy 17 upvotes, 4 comments
  22. A CPU that runs entirely on GPU 159 upvotes, 85 comments
  23. Nobody Gets Promoted for Simplicity 254 upvotes, 149 comments
  24. Weave – A language aware merge algorithm based on entities 159 upvotes, 91 comments
  25. Bet on German Train Delays 197 upvotes, 140 comments
  26. Glaze by Raycast 71 upvotes, 43 comments
  27. MacBook Neo 145 upvotes, 157 comments
  28. Apple Introduces MacBook Neo 146 upvotes, 165 comments
  29. The JVG algorithm could break RSA-2048 encryption with fewer than 5k qubits 8 upvotes, 7 comments
